attestation.attestation_note: Three corrections to the agent's extraction: (1) summary said 'Lead Engineer with 6+ years' — corrected to ~5 years full-time (2019-2024), SWE->Senior->Lead, plus a 2025 co-op (the draft rounded up across a gap and generalized the title); (2) removed skills I would not confidently defend in an interview (RLHF fine-tuning, Computer Vision, Multimodal AI) — they were in my skills file but not demonstrated in any role/project; (3) softened 'GuitarZero used by real users' to the verifiable 'publicly deployed'. Open: dollar/percent metrics below are my own prior-resume claims, kept as-is — I can source them in an interview.
attestation._confirm: Gaurav — review and keep attested:true only if you agree with the three corrections (esp. that you can't readily defend RLHF/CV/Multimodal). If you CAN defend any, tell the agent to re-add it.

person.name: Gaurav Bakale
person.work_authorization: F-1 OPT (authorized to work in the US now; will need employer sponsorship, e.g. H-1B, after OPT)
person._sanitization_note: Phone, street address, and personal email intentionally omitted — this file is committed to a public fork.

summary: Software engineer with ~5 years of full-time experience (2019-2024), progressing Software Engineer -> Senior -> Lead at Impact Analytics, plus a 2025 AI-engineering co-op. Completing an M.S. in Software Engineering (AI specialization) at Northeastern University (expected Aug 2026); recent work building agentic LLM platforms with CrewAI, LangChain, and TypeScript/Node.js.

experience.title: AI Engineer (Co-op)
highlights: Built a modular multi-agent orchestration platform using CrewAI, LangChain, and LlamaIndex — configurable AI-agent deployment with tool-calling, memory modules, and RAG pipelines.
Developed RESTful microservices in TypeScript and Node.js exposing agent workflows as scalable APIs, with PostgreSQL-backed state management and event-driven task queuing.
Built an LLM-powered natural-language query engine over PostgreSQL and Snowflake, auto-generating governance and operational reports (~70% reduction in manual reporting effort).
Containerised agent services with Docker, deployed on GCP with CI/CD and structured logging (sub-500ms API response in production).

experience.title: Lead Software Engineer
highlights: Led architecture and delivery of a data-platform application (user-access management, ETL configuration, time reporting, real-time notifications) serving 500+ enterprise users.
Migrated Airflow from VMs to GKE with horizontal auto-scaling (~35% infra-cost reduction; deployment time hours -> minutes).
Led development of LSTM and Transformer-based demand-forecasting and promotion-optimisation models across multi-store retail networks.
Built a POC benchmarking BigQuery vs. Snowflake (ingestion throughput, query latency, TCO); recommendation adopted org-wide.

experience.title: Senior Software Engineer
highlights: Built Apache Beam / Dataflow pipelines ingesting multi-terabyte datasets from SQL and SFTP sources into cloud warehouses with fault-tolerant exactly-once semantics.
Designed a REST pattern for long-running async tasks backed by Google Cloud Tasks with webhook completion, eliminating timeout failures for complex ETL jobs.

experience.title: Software Engineer
highlights: Identified customer segments and optimised coupon strategies via an exponential-decay model across multi-store retail networks (~$2M annualised margin impact).
Created a GLM-based model estimating price/promotion elasticities incorporating product affinity, cannibalisation, store-traffic forecasts, and competitor pricing.

projects.name: GuitarZero — Real-Time Guitar Trainer
highlights: Built and deployed a real-time guitar trainer powered by a lightweight in-browser neural network that detects played notes from live audio; publicly deployed at guitarzero.vercel.app.

projects.name: PulseFrame — Autonomous AI News-Art Bot
highlights: Autonomous daily pipeline: a local LLM (Ollama) summarises trending news, generates a Stable Diffusion prompt, and auto-posts artwork to Instagram via the Graph API — orchestrated as a LangChain multi-agent workflow with failure recovery and Slack alerting.

details: Teaching Assistant, DAMG6210 Data Management & Database Design
Coursework: Neural Networks & Deep Learning, Linear Algebra, Algorithms (Stanford Online)
